text  
the restaurant owner wants to transform their establishment into a tranquil oasis for diners. to achieve this, they plan to redesign the dining area and incorporate a small zen garden in the center. the dining area is currently a rectangular space measuring 20 meters in length and 15 meters in width.  

the zen garden will be a circular area with a radius of $ r $ meters, and it will be placed exactly in the center of the dining area. the owner wants the total area available for dining tables to be twice the area of the zen garden. calculate the radius $ r $ of the zen garden.  

the first step is to calculate the area of the dining area. the area of a rectangle is length multiplied by width, so $ 20 \times 15 = 300 $ square meters. the total area available for dining tables is twice the area of the zen garden, so $ 2 \times \pi r^2 $.  

the total area of the dining area is equal to the area of the zen garden plus the area available for dining tables. so, $ 300 = \pi r^2 + 2 \pi r^2 $. simplifying this equation gives $ 300 = 3 \pi r^2 $. solving for $ r^2 $, we get $ r^2 = \frac{300}{3 \pi} $. finally, $ r = \sqrt{\frac{300}{3 \pi}} $.  

the owner decides to install a small fountain at the center of the zen garden. the fountain will be a cylinder with a height of 1.5 meters and a radius that is one-third of the zen garden's radius. if the fountain is to be made of a material costing $50 per square meter, determine the total cost to cover the surface area of the cylindrical fountain (excluding the base area in contact with the zen garden).  

the radius of the fountain is $ \frac{r}{3} $. the surface area of a cylinder is $ 2 \pi r h $, where $ h $ is the height. substituting the values, the surface area is $ 2 \pi \times \frac{r}{3} \times 1.5 $. the total cost is $ 50 \times 2 \pi \times \frac{r}{3} \times 1.5 $.  

the answer is $ \text{text} $  
the first
